name: Vasilopita (New Year Bread)
recipeInstructions:
New Year Bread is traditionally cut at midnight on New Year's Eve. After baking, a coin is inserted through a slit in the base. The person who finds the coin will have luck in the New Year. Long ago the coin was a gold one, then later a silver coin was used. Nowadays, due to the nickel content of coins, it is undesirable to bake a coin in the cake.
1. Dissolve yeast in 1/4 cup of lukewarm milk.
2. Add remaining milk, beaten eggs, orange rind, and sugar to the yeast mixture.
3. Sift flour, salt, cinnamon, and masticha into a warm bowl, making a well in the center.
4. Pour the yeast mixture into the well and stir to combine with the flour.
5. Gradually add melted butter, mixing until the dough comes away from the sides.
6. Turn onto a floured surface and knead until smooth and elastic, adding more flour as needed.
7. Knead for 10 minutes. Place the ball of dough in a bowl coated with melted butter, turning to coat the top.
8. Cover with a cloth or plastic wrap and let rise in a warm place until doubled in bulk.
9. Punch down the dough and turn onto a lightly floured surface.
10. Knead lightly and shape into a round loaf.
11. Place in a greased baking sheet or 25 cm (10 inch) deep cake tin.
12. Cover and let rise again in a warm place until doubled, about 1 1/2 to 2 hours.
13. Glaze with beaten egg and arrange blanched almonds to represent the New Year.
14. Bake in a moderately hot oven for 45 minutes until golden brown and cooked when tested.
15. If the bread browns too quickly, place a piece of greased paper on top.
16. Cool on a wire rack.
